title: Track uses of TODO tags
description: Developers often use TODO tags to mark areas in the code where additional work or improvements are needed but are not implemented immediately. However, these TODO tags sometimes get overlooked or forgotten, leading to incomplete or unfinished code. This rule aims to identify and address unattended TODO tags to ensure a clean and maintainable codebase.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

noncompliant:
// TODO: refactor this code
public void ProcessData() { }

checks.message: TODO tag found - this indicates incomplete or planned work that should be addressed.

never_trigger_on: TODO in string literals (pattern only matches comments)
logic.reasoning: Developers use TODO tags to mark areas where additional work or improvements are needed. However, these tags sometimes get overlooked, leading to incomplete code. This rule tracks TODO tags to ensure they are addressed and the codebase remains clean and maintainable.
